english: (those of holy men are of a goodly size, long and stately) Stg. — bdun-prág (bdun phrag) (ἑβδομάς) seven days, a week, S.g. — *dún-na-tse* (*dun na tse*) W. a child born before the natural time, a seven months' child.
Sch. to belong to a class (?).

vb., adj., sbst., bdé-po (bde po) adj. Mil., C. (of rare occurrence), bdé-mo (bde mo) adj., col., esp. W., 1. to be happy or well; happy; happiness; mi bdé-ba (mi bde ba) the contrary of bdé-ba (bde ba); ṅa bdé-ste (nga bde ste) as I am quite happy Dzl.; bdeo (bdeo) he is happy, prospers, flourishes; bdé-bar byéd-pa (bde bar byed pa) to make happy; bdé-bar pẏin-pa (bde bar phyin pa) to come to a state of happiness, of rest, to a place of safety; bdé-bar gnás-pa (bde bar gnas pa) to be happy, to live in prosperity; bdé-bar gtóṅ-ba (bde bar gtong ba) to let alone, to let another be happy; kẏod bdé-bar btaṅ mi yoṅ we (khyod bde bar btang mi yong we) shall not allow you to be quiet Mil.; in C. col.: *žo* (*zho*) dé-mo-la mi žag* (*de mo la mi zhag*) id.; bdé-bar gyúr-čig (bde bar gyur cig), resp. bžúgs-šig (bzhugs shig), be happy! farewell! W. *dé-mo ča žig* (*de mo cha zhig*); bdé-bar btsá-ba (bde bar btsa' ba) B., *dé-mo-la kyé-če* (*de mo la kye che*) W., to be safely delivered of a child; bde-bar gšégs-pa (bde bar gshegs pa) he that has entered into eternal bliss, the blessed, Sch. (Köpp. I, 91?) an epithet of former Buddhas, Ssk. सुगत; lus daṅ sems mi-bdé-bar ogyúr-ba (lus dang sems mi bde bar 'gyur ba) to be bodily and spiritually afflicted Dzl.; mi-bdé-bai bágs-med-na (mi bde ba'i bags med na) fearless of adversity Dzl.; mi-bdé-bar ogyúr-ba (mi bde bar 'gyur ba) to ache, of parts of the body; mṅal mi-bdé-bar ogyúr-ba (mngal mi bde bar 'gyur ba) to be in travail, to suffer the pangs of childbirth; sems-bdé (sems bde), blo-bdé (blo bde), snyiṅ-bdé (snying bde) cheerful, merry, glad; šiṅ-tu ṭugs-ma-bdé-bar dắm-bčas-te (shing tu thugs ma bde bar dam bcas te) promising with a heavy heart, very reluctantly Glr.; dga-bdé (dga' bde) v. dgá-ba (dga' ba) comp.; dus-bdé (dus bde) (*-mo* (*-mo*) W.) peace, a state of peace, in C. frq. in conjunction with žod-ojágs (zhod 'jags) or sa-ojám (sa 'jam); ži(-bai)-bdé(-ba) (zhi ba'i bde ba) the happiness of rest, a happy tranquillity Glr.; peace Thgy.; esp. the happiness of Nirwana Thgy., Mil.; ojíg-rtén-gyi bdé-ba-la čhágs-te ('jig rten gyi bde ba la chags te) fond of a worldly life of pleasure Dzl.; bdé-ba daṅ ldán-pa (bde ba dang ldan pa) happy, bdé-ba-čan (bde ba can) v. below; ṅas-skábs-kyi bde-ba (ngas skabs kyi bde ba) a happy situation Glr.; mya-ṅán-las odás-pai bdé-ba tób-pa (mya ngan las 'das pa'i bde ba thob pa) to attain to the happiness of Nirwana Dzl.; dus-gtán-gyi bdé-ba-la bkod dgos (dus gtan gyi bde ba la bkod dgos) I must help him to attain to eternal bliss Mil.; ṕaṅ-bde (pang bde) v. ṕaṅ (pang). — 2. good, favourable, suited to its purpose ... na bdeo (na bdeo) (W. *dé-mo-yin* (*de mo yin*)) the best thing will be, if I... Dzl.; *gho dé-wa yoṅ* (*gho de wa yong*) C. so it becomes intelligible; good, well-qualified, well-adapted, kă lče bdé-ba (kha lce bde ba) with good organs of speech Pth.; smra-bdé-žiṅ (smra bde zhing) knowing to speak well, well-spoken Pth.; nyáms-rtogs-kyi smra lče bde (nyams rtogs kyi smra lce bde) a tongue skilled in speaking wisdom Pth.; in W. it is opp. to rtsóg-po (rtsog po): *lam de-mo* (*lam de mo*) the road is good, may be passed without risk. — 3. in W. bde (bde) is also the usual word for beautiful, more accurately: *(l)tá-na de-mo* (*lta na de mo*); *mī de-mo* (*mi de mo*) splendid indeed! *dé-mo man-na-méd* (*de mo man na med*) it is only for show Comp. bde-skyíd (bde skyid) happiness, felicity, frq.; bdé-gro (bde 'gro) going to happiness, joining the happy (spirits in heaven), also bdér-gro (bder 'gro), opp. to ṅán-gro (ngan 'gro); usually in a general sense, like our 'heaven'; bdé-gro mṭo-rís-kyi lus tób-pa (bde 'gro mtho ris kyi lus thob pa) to receive a heavenly (glorified) body. — bde-čén (bde chen) felicity, consummate bliss, frq. — bde-mčóg (bde mchog), शम्बर, सम्बर, a deity of more recent Buddhism, Schl. 108; Tar. — bde-ojágs (bde 'jags) prosperity, welfare. — bde-stóṅ (bde stong) (acc. to a Lama's statement for stabs bdé-ba (stabs bde ba), šes-ráb stoṅ-pa-nyíd (shes rab stong pa nyid)), an expression for contemplation, v. Was. (144 and 141). — bde-spyód (bde spyod) W., *de-čód* (*de chod*) C. *de-čộ* (*de cho*), col. euphemism for privy. — bdé-ba-čan (bde ba can) सुखावाति, bdé-ba-čan-gyi žiṅ-kams (bde ba can gyi zhing khams) the land of bliss, a sort of heaven or paradise, in the far west, the abode of Dhyani Buddha Amitabha, v. Glr. chapt. IV., Köpp. II., 27. — bde-byéd (bde byed) he who or that which makes happy Cs., शङ्कर. — bde-byúṅ (bde byung) शम्भु, शम्वस, source of happiness, n. of Siwa; as symb. num.: 11. — bde-blág (bde blag) ease, content Cs., acc. to our Lama: quickness, speed, nád-pa bde-blág-tu ysós-par ogyúr-bai mtsan-nyíd (nad pa bde blag tu gsos par 'gyur ba'i mtshan nyid) Wdn. a sign that the patient will soon recover. — bde-légs (bde legs) well-being; ...las bde-légs-su gyúr-čig (...las bde legs su gyur cig) they shall recover from ..., they shall prosper again after ... Dom.
I. vb. 1. to be true, and adj. true, kẏod zér-ba bdén-no (khyod zer ba bden no)
